documents.title: University of California, Los Angeles public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: UCLA combines centralized pilots and governance through DTS, the Amazon Science Hub, teaching policy resources, an AI advisory committee, Break Through Tech AI, and AIM-AHEAD health equity work.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: University of California, Los Angeles's public AI policy posture is comprehensive and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for CA. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: University of California, Los Angeles is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: UCLA combines centralized pilots and governance through DTS, the Amazon Science Hub, teaching policy resources, an AI advisory committee, Break Through Tech AI, and AIM-AHEAD health equity work. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were usually not public.
Follow-up search closed platform_usage_or_contracts at product/scope level for ChatGPT Enterprise, M365 Copilot and Gemini pilots, but no UCLA public user count or contract value was found.
Follow-up search closed assessment_redesign with UCLA teaching/course-policy guidance; campus AI spend and GPU capacity remain not publicly quantified in the searched sources.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: platform first.
